package_description: LED and controls retrofit bundle for commercial lighting systems, kept separate from broader electrification and HVAC pathway changes.
rollout_limit_notes: Lighting retrofits are mature and relatively fast to deploy, but keep the role-level cap below full stock saturation because the aggregate commercial role spans many building types and legacy starting points.
evidence_summary: Commercial lighting is one of the cleanest portable v1 efficiency packages because it is electricity-only, mature, and already supported by Australian programs and building guidance.
derivation_method: Apply a conservative electricity-only multiplier within the accepted research band so the package remains separable from the larger incumbent-to-electrified pathway-method jump.
review_notes: Deep-electric remains excluded because it already represents the role frontier.

package_description: Operational HVAC commissioning, scheduling, controls, and BMS optimisation on incumbent and partially electrified commercial building-service methods.
rollout_limit_notes: Operational controls can scale faster than capital retrofits, but persistence depends on ongoing management quality and building suitability. Keep the cap below universal uptake on the aggregate commercial role.
evidence_summary: Commercial HVAC optimisation is a strong operational package candidate because it is separable from route change and can materially reduce electricity and residual heating energy without creating a new technology method.
derivation_method: Use a conservative operational multiplier on electricity and residual gas so the package stays materially smaller than the pathway-method gap and shares a non-stacking group with the lighting retrofit.
review_notes: Do not apply to the deep-electric method. The shared non-stacking group prevents the model from stacking two broad commercial add-on packages on the same activity slice.
